description:
Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: test and qa, using the docker:stable image and docker:stable-dind service for the test jobs and setting DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2 and DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR='' variables. In the test stage add a code_quality job that allows failure, is skipped when $CODE_QUALITY_DISABLED is set, runs the script `if ! docker info &>/dev/null; then
  if [ -z "$DOCKER_HOST" -a "$KUBERNETES_PORT" ]; then
    export DOCKER_HOST='tcp://localhost:2375'
  fi
fi` followed by `docker run --env SOURCE_CODE="$PWD" --volume "$PWD":/code --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock "registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/codequality:12-0-stable" /code`, and always uploads gl-code-quality-report.json as a codequality artifact. Also in the test stage add a dependency_scanning job that allows failure, is skipped when $DEPENDENCY_SCANNING_DISABLED is set and runs only if $GITLAB_FEATURES matches dependency_scanning; its script exports DS_VERSION, runs the same docker‑info guard, defines a propagate_env_vars function, and executes `docker run $(propagate_env_vars DS_ANALYZER_IMAGES DS_ANALYZER_IMAGE_PREFIX DS_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TAG DS_DEFAULT_ANALYZERS DS_EXCLUDED_PATHS DEP_SCAN_DISABLE_REMOTE_CHECKS DS_DOCKER_CLIENT_NEGOTIATION_TIMEOUT DS_PULL_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TIMEOUT DS_RUN_ANALYZER_TIMEOUT DS_PYTHON_VERSION DS_PIP_DEPENDENCY_PATH PIP_INDEX_URL PIP_EXTRA_INDEX_URL) --volume "$PWD:/code" --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock "registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/dependency-scanning:$DS_VERSION" /code`, producing a gl-dependency-scanning-report.json artifact. Add a sast job in the test stage that allows failure, is skipped when $SAST_DISABLED is set and runs only if $GITLAB_FEATURES matches sast; its script exports SAST_VERSION, runs the docker‑info guard, defines a similar propagate_env_vars function with many SAST variables, and runs `docker run $(propagate_env_vars SAST_BANDIT_EXCLUDED_PATHS SAST_ANALYZER_IMAGES SAST_ANALYZER_IMAGE_PREFIX SAST_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TAG SAST_DEFAULT_ANALYZERS SAST_PULL_ANALYZER_IMAGES SAST_BRAKEMAN_LEVEL SAST_FLAWFINDER_LEVEL SAST_GITLEAKS_ENTROPY_LEVEL SAST_GOSEC_LEVEL SAST_EXCLUDED_PATHS SAST_DOCKER_CLIENT_NEGOTIATION_TIMEOUT SAST_PULL_ANALYZER_IMAGE_TIMEOUT SAST_RUN_ANALYZER_TIMEOUT SAST_JAVA_VERSION ANT_HOME ANT_PATH GRADLE_PATH JAVA_OPTS JAVA_PATH JAVA_8_VERSION JAVA_11_VERSION MAVEN_CLI_OPTS MAVEN_PATH MAVEN_REPO_PATH SBT_PATH FAIL_NEVER) --volume "$PWD:/code" --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock "registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/sast:$SAST_VERSION" /app/bin/run /code`, and uploads gl-sast-report.json as a sast artifact. In the qa stage add a dast job that allows failure, depends on the review-deploy job, runs only if $GITLAB_FEATURES matches dast and $DAST_DISABLED is not set, uses the image registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/dast:$CI_SERVER_VERSION_MAJOR-$CI_SERVER_VERSION_MINOR-stable, runs a before_script `export DAST_WEBSITE="$(cat review_app_url.txt)"` and then the script `export DAST_WEBSITE=${DAST_WEBSITE:-$(cat environment_url.txt)}` followed by `/analyze -t $DAST_WEBSITE`, and stores gl-dast-report.json as a dast artifact.
